articles.title: Moon milestones: A rundown of Artemis 2's many spaceflight firsts
articles.description: You've probably heard it by now: NASA’s Artemis 2 mission was the first to launch astronauts to fly by the moon in more than 50 years. But that was just the first of the firsts for this mission.

articles.title: 'This is really big stuff.' Trump hails NASA Artemis 2 astronauts after historic moon flyby feat
articles.description: President Donald Trump on Monday night (April 6) hailed the courage and grit of NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts after their historic flyby around the moon.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 6: Crew Wraps Historic Lunar Flyby
articles.description: The Artemis II mission wrapped up a historic seven-hour lunar flyby, marking humanity’s first return to the Moon since Apollo 17 in 1972 and capturing images of the lunar far side. After the lunar observation period concluded, the Artemis II crew was congratulated by President Donald J. Trump, in a

articles.title: Artemis 2 swings around the moon
articles.description: Four astronauts from the United States and Canada became the humans to travel the furthest from the Earth April 6 as they went around the moon on the Artemis 2 mission. The post Artemis 2 swings around the moon appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts fly around the moon in record-breaking lunar loop by NASA
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts viewed parts of the moon never before seen with human eyes during their epic lunar flyby today (April 6) —and set a spaceflight record in the process.

articles.title: Moon music: Here's a Spotify playlist of the Artemis 2 crew's wakeup songs
articles.description: Artemis 2 wake-up songs are mission control's way of starting the crew's day off with positivity. Here's the Spotify playlist.

articles.title: Moon memorial: Artemis 2 astronauts name lunar 'bright spot' after mission commander's late wife
articles.description: As they broke the record for farthest distance from Earth ever traveled by an astronaut, NASA's Artemis 2 crew took a moment to dedicate a few features on the moon's surface for their spacecraft and lost loved ones.

articles.title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight
articles.description: Editor’s note: This release was updated at 7:28 p.m. EDT on April 6, 2026, to reflect Orion’s final closest approach to the lunar surface from about 4,070 miles to about 4,067 miles. articles.title: Artemis 2 breaks humanity's all-time distance record during historic loop around the moon (video)
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts have now traveled farther from Earth than any people in history, breaking the mark set by the Apollo 13 crew in April 1970.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 6: Lunar Flyby Updates
articles.description: NASA’s live coverage of the Artemis II lunar flyby is underway on NASA+, Amazon Prime, Apple TV, Hulu, Netflix, HBO Max, and Roku, alongside the agency’s 24/7 coverage on its YouTube channel. Learn how to stream NASA content through a variety of online platforms, including social media.

articles.title: The moon up close: How the Artemis 2 astronauts are photographing their historic lunar flyby
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts might be the first humans to survey certain areas of the moon's far side, and they're chronicling their historic journey in detail.

articles.title: Night Sky Network Celebrates Artemis II
articles.description: Today, the crew of Artemis II reaches a milestone, traveling farther than any humans in recorded history, as they orbit the far side of the Moon, roughly 4,000 miles above the lunar surface. You can watch this historic broadcast on NASA’s YouTube channel today, April 6, 2026, at 10 AM Pacific (1 PM

articles.title: The Near Side of the Moon
articles.description: The astronauts aboard NASA’s Orion spacecraft captured this photo of the Moon’s near side on April 4, 2026. articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 6: Crew Ready for Lunar Flyby
articles.description: The Artemis II crew is awake and beginning preparations for today’s lunar flyby — a first for humans since Apollo 17 in 1972. articles.title: Astronaut Victor Glover is the latest in a long line of Black American explorers − including York, the enslaved man who played a key role in the Lewis and Clark expedition
articles.description: Glover's achievement is worth celebrating. But it's also worth remembering that he belongs to a long and underappreciated history.

articles.title: Artemis 2 ready to fly around the moon
articles.description: The Artemis 2 mission will swing around the moon April 6, setting a distance record as astronauts study part of the lunar farside. The post Artemis 2 ready to fly around the moon appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA Artemis 2 astronauts to make historic moon flyby today. Here's what to expect hour by hour (timeline)
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts will make a historic moon flyby today (April 6). Here's how it will happen, hour by hour.

articles.title: Humanity is back at the moon! Artemis 2 astronauts arrive in lunar space
articles.description: The four astronauts of NASA's Artemis 2 mission have arrived at the moon. They entered the lunar sphere of influence early Monday morning (April 6).

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 5: Correction Burn Complete
articles.description: Mission control teams in Houston and the Artemis II crew completed an outbound correction burn to refine the Orion spacecraft’s trajectory to the Moon. The burn began at 11:03 p.m. EDT and lasted 17.5 seconds. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christ

articles.title: When the moon hits your eye from your Orion ship up high, that's a 'mare' for Artemis 2
articles.description: The four Artemis 2 astronauts had a chance to observe the moon this weekend, and to say they were thrilled may be an understatement of cosmic proportions.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 5: Crew Demos Suits, Readies for Lunar Flyby
articles.description: The Artemis II crew of NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen, continue their workday aboard the Orion spacecraft.

articles.title: NASA stops work on SLS Mobile Launcher 2
articles.description: NASA has stopped work on a second mobile launch platform intended for an upgraded version of the Space Launch System the agency no longer plans to develop. The post NASA stops work on SLS Mobile Launcher 2 appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts are about to see one of the rarest skywatching sights of all — a solar eclipse from beyond the moon
articles.description: The four Artemis 2 astronauts will see a solar eclipse from beyond the moon's far side on Monday evening (April 6), and they'll use the opportunity to make some science observations.

articles.title: The breakout star of NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission isn't an astronaut — it's the space toilet
articles.description: The on-again, off-again lunar loo allows astronauts to "go" at the moon like no one's gone before.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 5: Crew Starts Day with Suit Demo
articles.description: The morning opened to the beat of “Working Class Heroes (Work)” by CeeLo Green as the Artemis II crew, now flying about 65,235 miles from the Moon, began preparations for their first test objective: an evaluation of the Orion Crew Survival System suit. articles.title: Thinking of You, Earth
articles.description: On April 4, 2026, NASA astronaut and Artemis II Commander Reid Wiseman peers out of one of the Orion spacecraft’s main cabin windows, looking back at Earth, as the crew travels towards the Moon. The Artemis II astronauts – Wiseman and fellow NASA astronauts Christina Koch and Victor Glover, an

articles.title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby
articles.description: Plans are in place for the crew of Artemis 2 to try to replicate one of the most famous images ever taken from space — Apollo 8's shot of Earth rising over the moon's horizon.

articles.title: This is the inspiring Easter message an Artemis 2 astronaut has for Earth while traveling to the moon (video)
articles.description: NASA astronaut Victor Glover delivered an impromptu Easter message as his Artemis 2 Orion capsule sped toward the moon on Saturday (April 4).

articles.title: NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby
articles.description: As Artemis 2 approaches the moon, a NASA science team is finalizing plans for the observations they want the astronauts to perform during their flyby. The post NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 4: Crew Completes Manual Piloting Demonstration
articles.description: NASA astronaut Christina Koch, left, takes control of the Orion spacecraft during a manual piloting test on flight day 4 of the Artemis II mission. To her right is CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen and NASA astronaut Victor Glover.

articles.description: As the Orion spacecraft continues its path toward the Moon, the Artemis II crew will spend their fourth flight day preparing for their lunar flyby on Monday, April 6. articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts — now halfway to the moon — report 'burning smell' from toilet, but everything's fine
articles.description: The astronauts on NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission reported a burning smell from their Orion spacecraft toilet overnight, but Mission Control isn't overly concerned. They are over halfway to the moon today.

articles.title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 204 — A New NASA
articles.description: On Episode 204 of This Week In Space, Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik bring it to you from Houston, as the Artemis 2 mission continues.

articles.title: Illuminated in Orion
articles.description: NASA astronaut Christina Koch reads on a tablet in the dimly lit Orion crew capsule in this April 3, 2026, photo. articles.title: An Artemis 2 astronaut took a 'bath' on camera on the way to the moon. Mission Control's reaction was priceless (video)
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 pilot Victor Glover was cleaning up after a workout when the cameras caught a bit more than expected. Mission Control's response was priceless.

articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts are now headed to the moon. Why has it taken humanity so long to go back?
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts are the first people to journey toward the moon since 1972. Why has it taken us more than half a century to go back?

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 3: Crew Prepares Cabin for Lunar Flyby
articles.description: The Artemis II mission is more than halfway to the Moon. articles.title: Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon
articles.description: A day after lighting its engine to head to the moon, the Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is performing well with only minor issues. The post Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: It's official: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will break humanity's all-time distance record
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will get a maximum of 252,757 miles (406,773 kilometers) from Earth on April 6, breaking the mark set by Apollo 13 in April 1970.

articles.title: Astronauts on the ISS watched NASA's historic Artemis 2 launch from space
articles.description: From the ground to low Earth orbit, the Artemis 2 launch became a shared moment across the planet and beyond.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 3: Outbound Trajectory Correction Burn Update
articles.description: NASA’s Artemis II crew in Orion continues on a precise trajectory to flyby the Moon on Monday, April 6. articles.title: 1st results from Blue Ghost lunar lander reveal how much we still don't know about the moon
articles.description: Data from Firefly Aerospace's Blue Ghost lunar lander casts doubt on the decades-old view of the moon as divided between a hotter near side and cooler far side.

articles.title: Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand
articles.description: Italy’s Argotec has officially opened its first U.S. satellite production facility, cementing a foothold near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to join other foreign space firms pursuing growing demand from American programs. articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts see Earth in the rear-view mirror | Space photo of the day for April 3, 2026
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts snapped a gorgeous photo of their home planet recently as they sped away from it and toward the moon.

articles.title: NASA is sending astronauts back to the moon. Can you see the Artemis 4 landing sites from Earth?
articles.description: NASA's Artemis Program seeks to place American boots close to the moon's south pole.

articles.title: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are cruising to the moon. So why are they doing CPR tests today?
articles.description: After an action-packed first two days, Flight Day 3 may bring a more relaxed pace for the Artemis 2 moon astronauts.
